text: I am, I exist — that is certain.
I am, then, in the strict sense only a thing that thinks.
Cogito, ergo sum.
It must be possible for the 'I think' to accompany all my representations.
No representation can exist for me.
Consciousness always accompanies thinking.
As far as this consciousness can be extended backwards, so far reaches the identity of that person.
It is the same self now it was then.
I never can catch myself at any time without a perception.
ref: Hume, A Treatise of Human Nature

text: I never can observe any thing but the perception.
ref: Hume, A Treatise of Human Nature

text: When my perceptions are removed for any time, I am insensible of myself.
ref: Hume, A Treatise of Human Nature

text: Within each personal consciousness thought is sensibly continuous.
ref: William James, The Principles of Psychology

text: Within each personal consciousness thought is always changing.
ref: William James, The Principles of Psychology

text: Consciousness is the last and latest development of the organic.
Body am I entirely, and nothing more.
Soul is only the name of something in the body.
Le moi est haïssable.
The self is a relation which relates itself to its own self.
Man is spirit. But what is spirit? Spirit is the self.
Their Esse is Percipi.
ref: Berkeley, A Treatise Concerning the Principles of Human Knowledge

text: The human mind is the very idea or knowledge of the human body.
Some things are in our control and others not.
Let us flee then to the beloved Fatherland.
The Fatherland to us is There whence we have come.
I come to the fields and spacious palaces of my memory.
What is nearer to me than myself?
This is not mine, this is not I, this is not my self.
Consciousness is not self.
Tat tvam asi.
Thou art That.
昔者莊周夢為胡蝶。栩栩然胡蝶也。
不知周之夢為胡蝶與，胡蝶之夢為周與。
周與胡蝶，則必有分矣。此之謂物化。
今者吾喪我，汝知之乎？
知人者智，自知者明。
勝人者有力，自勝者強。
吾日三省吾身。
盡其心者，知其性也。
知其性，則知天矣。
Trust thyself: every heart vibrates to that iron string.
Nothing is at last sacred but the integrity of your own mind.
Que sais-je?
If the doors of perception were cleansed every thing would appear to man as it is, infinite.
ref: William Blake, The Marriage of Heaven and Hell

text: For man has closed himself up, till he sees all things thro' narrow chinks of his cavern.
ref: William Blake, The Marriage of Heaven and Hell

text: I celebrate myself, and sing myself.
For every atom belonging to me as good belongs to you.
I loafe and invite my soul.
Do I contradict myself? Very well then I contradict myself.
I am large, I contain multitudes.
I'm Nobody! Who are you?
ref: Emily Dickinson, I'm Nobody! Who are you?

text: Are you — Nobody — too?
ref: Emily Dickinson, I'm Nobody! Who are you?

text: How dreary — to be — Somebody!
ref: Emily Dickinson, I'm Nobody! Who are you?

text: How public — like a Frog —
ref: Emily Dickinson, I'm Nobody! Who are you?

text: The Brain—is wider than the Sky—
ref: Emily Dickinson, The Brain—is wider than the Sky

text: The one the other will contain.
ref: Emily Dickinson, The Brain—is wider than the Sky

text: The Brain is deeper than the sea—
ref: Emily Dickinson, The Brain—is wider than the Sky

text: Car Je est un autre.
J’assiste à l’éclosion de ma pensée.
Zwei Seelen wohnen, ach! in meiner Brust.
Die eine will sich von der andern trennen.
Denn da ist keine Stelle, die dich nicht sieht.
Du mußt dein Leben ändern.
Who is it that can tell me who I am?
Lear's shadow.
What a piece of work is a man!
How noble in reason! how infinite in faculty!
And yet, to me, what is this quintessence of dust?
Life's but a walking shadow.
It is a tale told by an idiot, full of sound and fury, signifying nothing.
We are such stuff as dreams are made on.
Our little life is rounded with a sleep.
Nach Innen geht der geheimnisvolle Weg.
In uns, oder nirgends ist die Ewigkeit mit ihren Welten.
Die Tiefen unseres Geistes kennen wir nicht.
Ein Zeichen sind wir, deutungslos.
Schmerzlos sind wir und haben fast die Sprache in der Fremde verloren.
Não sou nada.
ref: Fernando Pessoa / Álvaro de Campos, Tabacaria

text: Nunca serei nada.
ref: Fernando Pessoa / Álvaro de Campos, Tabacaria

text: À parte isso, tenho em mim todos os sonhos do mundo.
ref: Fernando Pessoa / Álvaro de Campos, Tabacaria

text: Wo Es war, soll Ich werden.
